TM001(02) Flashing Light Video Monitor Assembly Instructions Materials Required Procedure Kitting Instructions FLVM Assembly Instructions 2-4-2026.docx Materials Required 1. 18 AWG 90C wire (red and black) 2. Zip ties 3. Label maker 4. WAGO 221-415 5-position lever nuts 5. WAGO 221-612 2-position lever nuts (10 AWG) 6. Wire cutter/snips 7. 3/32' hex drive bit 8. 1/8' hex drive bit 9. 5/32 hex drive bit 10. Precision flat head screwdriver Small and large Phillips screwdriver Procedure 1. Drill holes in enclosure for antenna, 2x vents, and 1' NPT conduit fitting. Install the antenna base into the top enclosure hole. Be sure to lubricate the o-ring before tightening.

DC005[W] FLVM Wire Lengths The following wire lengths are recommended for the assembly of the Flashing Light View Monitor electronics enclosure. Lengths are permitted to be adjusted by the production team to improve routing and assembly. All names are the same as are used on the wiring diagram. The only exception are the highlighted wires in Figure 1 below. These will be referred to as BATT JUMPER BLACK and BATT JUMPER RED in the wire length table. Figure 1 - Batt jumper wires 18AWG, 90C, RED Wires

question: Where is the Netgear Switch located in the electronics enclosure diagram?
ground_truth: The Netgear Switch is located at the top right corner of the electronics enclosure.

text_chunks: Install vents on left and right side of enclosure. Place aluminum plate on bench. Attach Netgear switch using zip ties in top right of plate. The CAT5 ports will point to the left.

Complete power wiring for Netgear switch with one of the barrel jack connectors. Connect the barrel jack into the netgear switch and the other end to the distribution block (the wire you marked with red goes into the + distribution block and the other goes in the - distribution block). Complete power wiring for PoE Injector . Leave extra slack in the PoE injector positive wire as it will be split for the web relay later.

NOTES: 1. USE 18AWG 90°C WIRE UNLESS OTHERWISE SPECIFIED. Install 1FT CAT5 cable between PoE Data IN port and Netgear switch. Install web relay to hex standoffs on top-right of plate using four 4-40 socket head cap screws. Complete power wiring for web relay by installing second barrel jack connector.

TURN OFF ALL BREAKERS. 26. Complete continuity check procedure DC004. Attach modem to mezzanine plate using QTY two (2) 10-32 socket head cap screws. Attach 10FT outdoor ethernet cable to DATA OUT of PoE injector and coil inside enclosure. Retain with zip tie for shipping.

question: What is the expected resistance between the Solar Controller Batt + and Batt - points?

text_chunks: Complete power wiring for the solar controller BATT input. This includes the BATT+, BATT-, and BATT +_SW wiring from the wiring diagram. 15. Complete power wiring for solar controller PV input . This includes the PV +, PV -, and PV+_SW wiring from the wiring diagram. Ensure that you use 10AWG (PN 221-612) WAGO connectors on the PV pigtails. The pigtails should be at least 12' long.

Before placing the solar charge controller on the plate, make a note of the factory pin and serial number printed on the label on the top of the device. This will be used later to configure settings with the VictronConnect app. Attach solar charge controller to top left of plate using QTY four (4) 6-32 socket head cap screws. Attach PoE injector to middle left of plate using QTY four (4) 4-40 phillips truss head screws.

Complete wiring for solar controller LOAD output . This includes the LOAD +, LOAD -, LOAD +_SW, and J+ wiring from the schematic. Use a DMM to check the polarity of the two barrel jack connectors. The outside of the barrel jack is negative. Mark the positive wire with a piece of red tape.

text_chunks: Split PoE Injector wire and connect to web relay Relay 1. The C terminal goes to the distribution block. The NO terminal goes to the PoE injector. Complete the power wiring for the modem cable. The white , red, and green wires go to the positive distribution block. The black wire goes to the negative distribution block.

Complete power wiring for Netgear switch with one of the barrel jack connectors. Connect the barrel jack into the netgear switch and the other end to the distribution block (the wire you marked with red goes into the + distribution block and the other goes in the - distribution block). Complete power wiring for PoE Injector . Leave extra slack in the PoE injector positive wire as it will be split for the web relay later.

Wrap the strap around the aluminum plate and place the aluminum plate into the enclosure. Attach with included mounting screws. Secure mounting strap by buckling it around the plate. Connect antenna to CELLULAR SMA jack on modem and tighten with a pliers or wrench. Apply a zip tie and label with 'PV IN' around the PV+/- pigtail wires. Attach 10FT outdoor ethernet cable to DATA OUT of PoE injector and coil inside enclosure. Retain with zip tie for shipping.

Before placing the solar charge controller on the plate, make a note of the factory pin and serial number printed on the label on the top of the device. This will be used later to configure settings with the VictronConnect app. Attach solar charge controller to top left of plate using QTY four (4) 6-32 socket head cap screws. Attach PoE injector to middle left of plate using QTY four (4) 4-40 phillips truss head screws.

text_chunks: Split PoE Injector wire and connect to web relay Relay 1. The C terminal goes to the distribution block. The NO terminal goes to the PoE injector. Complete the power wiring for the modem cable. The white , red, and green wires go to the positive distribution block. The black wire goes to the negative distribution block.

Complete power wiring for Netgear switch with one of the barrel jack connectors. Connect the barrel jack into the netgear switch and the other end to the distribution block (the wire you marked with red goes into the + distribution block and the other goes in the - distribution block). Complete power wiring for PoE Injector . Leave extra slack in the PoE injector positive wire as it will be split for the web relay later.

Load the WAGO DIN rail carriers which will function as distribution blocks. Install two (2) 5position WAGOs (PN 221-415) into one carrier (PN 221-501) and a single (1) 5-position WAGO into the second carrier. The WAGOs should audibly snap into the DIN carrier. Snap the WAGO distribution blocks onto the left side of the DIN rail. Mark the block with two WAGOs as '+' and the other as '-' using a permanent marker. Install QTY eight (8) 2' x 4-40 hex standoffs for web relay and modem mezzanine plate.

text_chunks: Install vents on left and right side of enclosure. Place aluminum plate on bench. Attach Netgear switch using zip ties in top right of plate. The CAT5 ports will point to the left.

TURN OFF ALL BREAKERS. 26. Complete continuity check procedure DC004. Attach modem to mezzanine plate using QTY two (2) 10-32 socket head cap screws. Attach modem to 4-40 hex standoffs using QTY four 4-40 socket head cap screws. Install CAT5 cables between the modem, web relay, and the Netgear switch. Attach power cable to modem

Complete power wiring for Netgear switch with one of the barrel jack connectors. Connect the barrel jack into the netgear switch and the other end to the distribution block (the wire you marked with red goes into the + distribution block and the other goes in the - distribution block). Complete power wiring for PoE Injector . Leave extra slack in the PoE injector positive wire as it will be split for the web relay later.

text_chunks: Before placing the solar charge controller on the plate, make a note of the factory pin and serial number printed on the label on the top of the device. This will be used later to configure settings with the VictronConnect app. Attach solar charge controller to top left of plate using QTY four (4) 6-32 socket head cap screws. Attach PoE injector to middle left of plate using QTY four (4) 4-40 phillips truss head screws.

A list of devices will be displayed (you may be asked to turn on Bluetooth). Select the device based on the serial number that was noted during production. If this is not available, the serial number and factory pin can be found on top of the solar controller. Pair with the device when prompted by using the factory pin.

Complete wiring for solar controller LOAD output . This includes the LOAD +, LOAD -, LOAD +_SW, and J+ wiring from the schematic. Use a DMM to check the polarity of the two barrel jack connectors. The outside of the barrel jack is negative. Mark the positive wire with a piece of red tape.

text_chunks: Attach DIN rail to middle right of plate using QTY four (4) 10-32 hex button head screws. Attach circuit breakers to DIN rail. From left to right, the order is 6A -> 13A -> 2A Mark the circuit breakers , from left to right, as PV --> BATT --> LOAD using a permanent marker.

Load the WAGO DIN rail carriers which will function as distribution blocks. Install two (2) 5position WAGOs (PN 221-415) into one carrier (PN 221-501) and a single (1) 5-position WAGO into the second carrier. The WAGOs should audibly snap into the DIN carrier. Snap the WAGO distribution blocks onto the left side of the DIN rail. Mark the block with two WAGOs as '+' and the other as '-' using a permanent marker. Install QTY eight (8) 2' x 4-40 hex standoffs for web relay and modem mezzanine plate.

Before placing the solar charge controller on the plate, make a note of the factory pin and serial number printed on the label on the top of the device. This will be used later to configure settings with the VictronConnect app. Attach solar charge controller to top left of plate using QTY four (4) 6-32 socket head cap screws. Attach PoE injector to middle left of plate using QTY four (4) 4-40 phillips truss head screws.

Attach DIN rail to middle right of plate using QTY four (4) 10-32 hex button head screws. Attach circuit breakers to DIN rail. From left to right, the order is 6A -> 13A -> 2A Mark the circuit breakers , from left to right, as PV --> BATT --> LOAD using a permanent marker.

question: What is the quantity of 10-32 socket head cap screws needed to attach the modem to the mezzanine plate?

text_chunks: TURN OFF ALL BREAKERS. 26. Complete continuity check procedure DC004. Attach modem to mezzanine plate using QTY two (2) 10-32 socket head cap screws. Attach modem to 4-40 hex standoffs using QTY four 4-40 socket head cap screws. Install CAT5 cables between the modem, web relay, and the Netgear switch. Attach power cable to modem

Attach DIN rail to middle right of plate using QTY four (4) 10-32 hex button head screws. Attach circuit breakers to DIN rail. From left to right, the order is 6A -> 13A -> 2A Mark the circuit breakers , from left to right, as PV --> BATT --> LOAD using a permanent marker.

Install 1FT CAT5 cable between PoE Data IN port and Netgear switch. Install web relay to hex standoffs on top-right of plate using four 4-40 socket head cap screws. Complete power wiring for web relay by installing second barrel jack connector.

Load the WAGO DIN rail carriers which will function as distribution blocks. Install two (2) 5position WAGOs (PN 221-415) into one carrier (PN 221-501) and a single (1) 5-position WAGO into the second carrier. The WAGOs should audibly snap into the DIN carrier. Snap the WAGO distribution blocks onto the left side of the DIN rail. Mark the block with two WAGOs as '+' and the other as '-' using a permanent marker. Install QTY eight (8) 2' x 4-40 hex standoffs for web relay and modem mezzanine plate.

Wrap the strap around the aluminum plate and place the aluminum plate into the enclosure. Attach with included mounting screws. Secure mounting strap by buckling it around the plate. Connect antenna to CELLULAR SMA jack on modem and tighten with a pliers or wrench. Apply a zip tie and label with 'PV IN' around the PV+/- pigtail wires. Attach 10FT outdoor ethernet cable to DATA OUT of PoE injector and coil inside enclosure. Retain with zip tie for shipping.

Wrap the strap around the aluminum plate and place the aluminum plate into the enclosure. Attach with included mounting screws. Secure mounting strap by buckling it around the plate. Connect antenna to CELLULAR SMA jack on modem and tighten with a pliers or wrench. Apply a zip tie and label with 'PV IN' around the PV+/- pigtail wires. Attach 10FT outdoor ethernet cable to DATA OUT of PoE injector and coil inside enclosure. Retain with zip tie for shipping.
Install vents on left and right side of enclosure. Place aluminum plate on bench. Attach Netgear switch using zip ties in top right of plate. The CAT5 ports will point to the left.

Load the WAGO DIN rail carriers which will function as distribution blocks. Install two (2) 5position WAGOs (PN 221-415) into one carrier (PN 221-501) and a single (1) 5-position WAGO into the second carrier. The WAGOs should audibly snap into the DIN carrier. Snap the WAGO distribution blocks onto the left side of the DIN rail. Mark the block with two WAGOs as '+' and the other as '-' using a permanent marker. Install QTY eight (8) 2' x 4-40 hex standoffs for web relay and modem mezzanine plate.

Load the WAGO DIN rail carriers which will function as distribution blocks. Install two (2) 5position WAGOs (PN 221-415) into one carrier (PN 221-501) and a single (1) 5-position WAGO into the second carrier. The WAGOs should audibly snap into the DIN carrier. Snap the WAGO distribution blocks onto the left side of the DIN rail. Mark the block with two WAGOs as '+' and the other as '-' using a permanent marker. question: Where can the factory pin be found?
ground_truth: On top of the solar controller.
predicted_answer: Answer: On top of the solar controller
raw_answer:
Answer: On top of the solar controller

Sources: [altumint_tm002_04_flvm_configuration_instructions_p07], [altumint_tm001_02_flashing_light_video_monitor_assembly_instructions_p03]

text_chunks: Return to the Settings screen. Select the three dots in the top right and tap Product Info . Under Pin Code , click Change. Enter the factory pin, then set the new pin as 460000 . Click OK.

A list of devices will be displayed (you may be asked to turn on Bluetooth). Select the device based on the serial number that was noted during production. If this is not available, the serial number and factory pin can be found on top of the solar controller. Pair with the device when prompted by using the factory pin.

Before placing the solar charge controller on the plate, make a note of the factory pin and serial number printed on the label on the top of the device. This will be used later to configure settings with the VictronConnect app. Attach solar charge controller to top left of plate using QTY four (4) 6-32 socket head cap screws. Attach PoE injector to middle left of plate using QTY four (4) 4-40 phillips truss head screws.

Attach DIN rail to middle right of plate using QTY four (4) 10-32 hex button head screws. Attach circuit breakers to DIN rail. From left to right, the order is 6A -> 13A -> 2A Mark the circuit breakers , from left to right, as PV --> BATT --> LOAD using a permanent marker.

question: What battery type should be selected under Battery Preset?

text_chunks: Tap the Settings Gear > Battery. Under Battery Preset, tap Select Preset, scroll down to select Smart Lithium (LiFePO4) and tap OK. Return to the Settings screen. Select Load Output . Confirm that under the Operation Mode menu the BatteryLife option is selected.

Complete power wiring for the solar controller BATT input. This includes the BATT+, BATT-, and BATT +_SW wiring from the wiring diagram. 15. Complete power wiring for solar controller PV input . This includes the PV +, PV -, and PV+_SW wiring from the wiring diagram. Ensure that you use 10AWG (PN 221-612) WAGO connectors on the PV pigtails. The pigtails should be at least 12' long.

Install vents on left and right side of enclosure. Place aluminum plate on bench. Attach Netgear switch using zip ties in top right of plate. The CAT5 ports will point to the left.

Split PoE Injector wire and connect to web relay Relay 1. The C terminal goes to the distribution block. The NO terminal goes to the PoE injector. Complete the power wiring for the modem cable. The white , red, and green wires go to the positive distribution block. The black wire goes to the negative distribution block.
